OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT SAFEWAY SUMMARY JUDGEMENT (TRANSACTION ID # 62665671) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRUBBS-FERGUSON, NEDRA , INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING.DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED AND ITS MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PREMISES LIABILITY AND GRANTED AS TO THE SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION FOR GROSS NEGLIGENCE AND NEGLIGENCE PER SE. (SEE MINI-MINUTES FOR DETAILS) PLAINTIFF HAS NOT SHOWN HOW FURTHER DISCOVERY WOULD REVEAL FACTS TO SUPPORT HER NEGLIGENCE PER SE AND GROSS NEGLIGENCE CAUSES OF ACTION. ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/HK)

DECLARATION OF MS. CHAU IN SUPPORT OF EX PARTE FOR ORDER FOR SHORTENING TIME TO FILE MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL AND REOPEN DISCOVERY (TRANSACTION ID # 62981044) FILED BY PLAINTIFF GRUBBS-FERGUSON, NEDRA , INDIVIDUAL

MASTER MOTION CALENDAR ON MAR-07-2019 IN 206, PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL DATE AND REOPEN DISCOVERY. WENDY H. CHAU, ESQ. APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. DAVID J. STREZA, ESQ. APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT. AFTER ARGUMENT AND FINDING NO GOOD CAUSE, THE COURT DENIES PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO CONTINUE TRIAL. THE TRIAL DATE OF 3/18/19 AT 9:30AM IN DEPT. 206 REMAINS. PLAINTIFF IS REQUIRED TO GET SPECIFIC DATES FOR THE EXPERT WITNESS TO BE AVAILABLE. DEFENDANT'S COUNSEL REQUESTS TO TRAIL 2 WEEKS DUE TO A PLANNED VACATION. THE COURT WILL TRAIL THE CASE. PARTIES TO WORK OUT A SCHEDULE THAT ACCOMMODATES THE DIFFERENT DATES AND TIMES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: GARRETT L. WONG; CLERK: R. VELUZ; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial Date
Off Calendar Mar-07-2019 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Defendant Safeway Inc.S Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Nov-27-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Wendy Chau, Esq. 415-539-6116 for Plaintiff.
Jonathan Varnica, Esq. 415-398-0200 of Vogl Meredith Burke LLP for Defendant.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the videos creates a triable issue. Ms. Ferguson fell/slipped on some liquid. Defendant argues the spill. Plaintiff argues gross negligence.The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Defendant Safeway Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied and its motion for summary adjudication is denied as to the first cause of action for premises liability and granted as to the second and third causes of action for gross negligence and negligence per se. Based on the video and the other evidence presented by Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson, there is a triable dispute whether Safeway had constructive knowledge of the spilled liquid. (Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1212 ("plaintiffs may demonstrate the storekeeper had constructive notice of the dangerous condition if they can show that the site had not been inspected within a reasonable period of time so that a person exercising due care would have discovered and corrected the hazard.")). Safeway is entitled to summary adjudication of the negligence per se cause of action because Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson failed to identify a statute which protected a class of persons including her from the type of harm which she suffered and whose violation was the cause of her injury. (Evidence Code 669). Safeway is entitled to a summary adjudication of the cause of action for gross negligence because Ms. Grubbs-Ferguson has failed to create a triable issue whether Safeway acted in an extreme departure from the standard of care or exhibited want of even scant care. Safeway was performing inspections at regular intervals and hired an employee whose only duty was to perform such inspections. Evidence that employees did not sign-in in person but had to log in on a computer, prior lawsuits against Safeway within the last five years, and Mr. Rose's declaration that he has often seen dirty floors and sometimes they are not cleaned for 30 minutes does not show an extreme departure from the standard of care. (Grebing v. 24 Hour Fitness USA, Inc. (2015) 234 Cal.App.4th 631, 639 (based on undisputed evidence that defendant "took several measures to ensure that its exercise equipment and facility were well maintained," the court found that "[defendant's conduct] cannot reasonably be regarded as demonstrating a want of even scant care or an extreme departure from the ordinary standard of conduct.")). Plaintiff has not shown how further discovery would reveal facts to support her negligence per se and gross negligence causes of action.

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
